topic: A person finds out their surprise party has been cancelled
raw_output:
[story 1]

The email landed in my inbox with a dull thud, immediately sinking into the stagnant pool of my afternoon. I reread the subject line three times: "Party Update." My fingers hovered over the keyboard, a strange, heavy stillness settling in my chest. The vibrant anticipation that had been humming beneath my skin moments before had abruptly sputtered out, leaving behind a cold, hollow ache. I stared out the window at the grey drizzle blurring the city lights, the silence of my apartment amplifying the sheer anticlimax of the news. I didn't move to check my phone again; the effort felt monumental.

[story 2]

A slow, sinking feeling began in my stomach as I processed the message. It wasn't anger, not exactly, but a profound sense of deflation. I let out a breath I hadn't realized I was holding, a shallow, ragged sound that scraped at my throat. I picked up the discarded wrapping paper from the last attempt at planning, tracing the creases with a fingertip. Every memory of the planned merriment—the laughter, the music, the sheer coordinated chaos—felt suddenly distant, like a dream that had dissolved upon waking. My shoulders slumped forward, the weight of the disappointment pressing down on my spine.

[story 3]

I reread the confirmation text, the words refusing to coalesce into anything positive. My gaze drifted from the screen to the meticulously decorated corner of the room, suddenly seeing the streamers and balloons as mocking remnants of a hope that had just evaporated. A tightness constricted around my jaw. I walked over to the window, pressing my forehead against the cool glass, trying to absorb some of the bleakness outside. There was no energy left for celebration; only a heavy inertia, a profound sense of emptiness where excitement used to reside. I just stood there, letting the quiet settle deep into my bones.
